economicInactivity5064.note: Economically inactive people aged 50-64, UK. Excludes those seeking work.

returnershipPlacements.note: Cumulative placements through the government Returnership scheme (apprenticeships, Skills Bootcamps, Sector-based Work Academy Programmes for over-50s).

illHealthInactivity.note: Percentage of economically inactive 50-64 year olds citing long-term illness or disability as main reason.

sources.name: ONS
sources.dataset: Labour Force Survey — Economic Inactivity by Age and Reason

metadata.methodology: Economic inactivity figures from LFS quarterly survey, April-June quarters for annual comparisons. Returnership placements are cumulative since scheme launch in spring 2022, as reported by DWP. Ill health reason from LFS reason for inactivity module.
knownIssues: Returnership scheme encompasses multiple existing programmes rebranded — true additionality is disputed
LFS reason for inactivity is self-reported and may conflate voluntary early retirement with health-driven exits
Pandemic disruption to LFS sampling affects 2020-2021 data quality
